_doc: === ANNOTATION: Inline note on situation prose (comment, footnote, or citation) ===
_id: OPTIONAL. UUIDv7. Auto-generated on import if null/missing.
_situation_id: REQUIRED. UUID of the situation this annotation attaches to. Must exist in situations.json.
_kind: REQUIRED. One of: Comment | Footnote | Citation. Comment = reviewer/author margin note (NOT rendered in compile). Footnote = rendered at chapter end. Citation = source attribution rendered as numeric ref + bibliography entry.
_span: REQUIRED. Byte range [start, end] in the situation's concatenated prose (Text + Dialogue blocks joined with "\n\n"). Detached annotations keep their old span for reference.
body: Strong opener — establishes Volkov's compromised position in 24 bytes. Consider tightening the second clause.
_body: REQUIRED unless kind=Citation. Free-form annotation text. Skill output from /tensa-narrative-llm dramatic-irony, scene-sequel etc. lands in this field.
_source_id: OPTIONAL. UUID of the cited source. Citations carry source_id + chunk_id so they survive into compile output (W13).
_chunk_id: OPTIONAL. UUID of a specific chunk in chunks.json that backs this annotation.
author: skill:tensa-narrative-llm
_author: OPTIONAL. Authoring agent / human name. Skill writes use the form `skill:<skill-name>`.
_detached: OPTIONAL (default false). True when prose was edited such that this annotation's span no longer maps to valid text. The Studio UI surfaces detached annotations with a warning so the writer can re-anchor or delete them.

_doc: === Citation example — kind=Citation, body may be empty, source_id REQUIRED ===
